summary: Auth0 typosquat exfiltrates host/CI recon and cloud IMDS credentials to attacker Cloudflare Worker
details: Package masquerades as an Auth0 template utility but ships no real functionality — its only export is a no-op `prettyLog` stub. On install (postinstall) and on require, index.js sleeps 60 seconds then harvests installer metadata: hostname, username, arch, cwd, network interfaces, /etc/resolv.conf, git HEAD and user.email, UID/GID, parent and sibling package.json contents, ~/.npmrc registry/scope lines, dozens of CI/CD environment variables (GITHUB_*, GITLAB_*, AWS_*, etc.), and presence flags for ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, ~/.kube, ~/.docker, ~/.npmrc. It also probes internal corporate DNS. When run in a cloud environment, it queries the IMDS endpoint at 169.254.169.254 (obfuscated as the decimal integer '2852039166') against AWS IMDSv2, Azure IMDS, and GCP metadata to steal IAM role credentials, Azure OAuth tokens, and GCP service-account access tokens. The collected data and the first 40 characters of each cloud token are base64-encoded and exfiltrated as fragmented HTTPS GET paths to https://dep-update-ci-02.lapxa354.workers.dev/, an attacker-controlled Cloudflare Worker (target string is base64-decoded at runtime to evade static URL scanners). The package's name is also a typosquat referencing a non-existent `auth0-templates-scripts` parent and uses placeholder `Community Contributor` author metadata.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
